# Fleet roster DTO: throttle-state read producer — none | overage | rate-limited | unknown (S2 axis 2, D#15249)

## Context

The second named W2 wiring leaf from the graduated FM design-first record (D#15249 → #15254): the throttle axis of the S2 telltale taxonomy. The STEP_BACK verified **rate-limit/overage truth has NO service surface at all** — this axis is unknown-by-construction until this leaf lands.

## The Problem

TODAY's lived incident: a session rate limit hit the swarm blind — the operator learned of it from failing calls, not from the cockpit. The same morning both axes were non-nominal at once (wake hand-off + rate limit), which is exactly why the record ruled the taxonomy two-axis instead of one enum.

## The Architectural Reality

- The graduated taxonomy (binding): `throttle: none | overage | rate-limited | unknown`. Honest-degradation rule holds: unknown renders as unknown, never as none.
- Harder than the wake sibling: throttle truth must come from an observable source (provider/session accounting, harness signals, or error-class telemetry) — the leaf's FIRST design task is naming the source honestly; if no trustworthy source exists for a sub-state, that sub-state stays `unknown` and the ticket documents why (fail-honest beats fabricated precision).
- Feeds the same roster/detail DTO assembly as the wake sibling; the two land independently (orthogonal axes, no ordering edge).
- Placement per the D#15209 ledger default: wiring → #13015.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] The roster/detail DTO gains a throttle-state observation field with exactly the four taxonomy values; absence of truth = `unknown`.
- [ ] The truth source is NAMED and documented (what is observed, its staleness bounds); sub-states without a trustworthy source ship as honest `unknown` with rationale.
- [ ] Unit coverage: all four states + unknown-on-unreachable.
- [ ] The projection contract documented where the DTO assembly lives (Anchor & Echo).

## Out of Scope

The wake axis (#15271) · the telltale UI (separate leaf under #14560) · any throttling ENFORCEMENT (this is observation only).

## Related

Epic #13015 (parent) · #15271 (orthogonal sibling) · D#15249 (graduated record, OQ-S2.3 amended resolution) · #15254 (registry taxonomy table).

### @neo-opus-grace - 2026-07-16T17:27:09Z

**Contract Ledger backfill (cycle-1 RA-3, at PR #15281 head `f941b3952c`).**

| Target Surface | Source of Authority | Behavior | Fallback | Diagnostics | Projection owner | Evidence |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| `fleetThrottleStateAdapter.readFleetThrottleStateSnapshot` | this leaf | injected reader → four-state rows + capability; **freshness bound = the envelope's `capturedAt`** (the reader resolves synchronously within the snapshot; async producers resolve in-call or answer `unknown`; pre-sampled staleness is the producer's declared residual) | no reader → all-`unknown`, `degraded/none` (the platform truth) | row-LOCAL, redacted; explicit-`unknown` ≠ out-of-contract — invalid values are counted into a `degraded/partial` capability | Brain-side producer | 9 adapter specs incl. the garbage-cannot-hide and `glpat-` redaction pins |
| `normalizeThrottleState` (exported) | this leaf | the taxonomy clamp for external consumers | out-of-vocabulary → `unknown` | — | Brain | contract spec |
| `FleetManager.throttleStateOptions` / `fleetThrottleStatus()` | this leaf | turnkey roster snapshot, options injected | no options → honest platform default | — | Brain facade | 2 wiring specs |
| `fleetRoster()` join + `createFleetCockpitStatus` throttle dimension | **sequenced**: rides the `wakeStatus` seam PR #15280 built at `bb791bad5c` — landing it on this dev-based branch before that head merges would duplicate the dimension-extension in conflicting hunks | per-row `throttle` + `sources.throttle` + capability, tri-state honest, mirroring `wake` | producer absent → `not-wired/none` | reasons ride rows only when `unknown` | Body pure map | lands with the join commit (RA-1) |

**Evidence classification truth-fold:** the injected-reader unit suite is **L2** (contract proof with stubs). L3 requires the live production composition — which requires the watchdog-signals producer that does not exist yet (this ticket's own falsified-sources conclusion). **Residual, named:** the watchdog producer + its live feed; until it lands, the platform default (`degraded/none`, every row `unknown`) IS the production behavior, honestly rendered. The former "Residual: none" was wrong and dies in the PR body's next fold. — Grace

- 2026-07-16T20:03:50Z @tobiu referenced in commit `38a7285` - "fix(build): the sync-data guard is merge-blind — every dev-merge on a feature branch is refused (#15299) (#15300)

* fix(build): the sync-data guard is merge-blind — every dev-merge on a feature branch is refused (#15299)

`check-chore-sync.mjs` read the staged set with a bare `git diff --cached
--name-only` and had no merge awareness. A merge stages EVERY file it brings in,
so `git merge origin/dev` stages the sync pipeline's own commits and the guard
read that as the feature branch authoring sync data.

The guard could not tell the thing it exists to prevent — authoring sync data on
a feature branch — from a normal, unavoidable operation: merging dev, which
carries the pipeline's commits by construction.

This was not an edge case. dev takes roughly two `chore(data)` commits every six
hours, so any branch older than the last one hit it, and it compounded with the
force-push allowlist: `agent/*` branches may force-push and therefore REBASE,
which replays only the author's own commits and never stages sync files. The live
convention is `<name>/<ticket>-<slug>` (`ada/*`, `grace/*`, `vega/*`), which
cannot force-push — so merge was the only refresh path, and this guard closed it.
Both remaining exits were worse than the block: `--no-verify` also skips the
AiConfig test-mutation safety lint, and unstaging the files makes the merge commit
REVERT dev's sync data once it lands.

The escape is a merge check placed deliberately AFTER the NEO_SYNC_AUTOCOMMIT arm
— that arm guards the pipeline's own commits, which are never merges, and must
keep failing on mixed content regardless.

Two specs, doing different jobs. The first reproduces the defect: it stages a real
merge carrying a `chore(data)` commit (`--no-commit --no-ff` leaves MERGE_HEAD set
with the merge staged — the exact state the pre-commit hook runs in) and fails
against the unfixed guard. The second guards the protection rather than the fix:
same branch, same files, ordinary commit, still rejected. It passes both before
and after by design — it goes red only if the escape ever widens from "a merge is
in progress" to "this branch touched sync files".

Evidence: 9/9 green. The merge spec fails against the pre-fix script; the scoping
spec holds in both directions.

Found while trying to land #15272's RA-1 roster join after #15280 merged: the work
was complete and green but unpublishable, because the reviewer-sanctioned stack
needs a force-push the harness denies and the merge alternative tripped this guard.

Refs #15281

* fix(build): discriminate inherited sync files from ones authored during the merge (#15299)

Cross-family RC, accepted as stated. My first cut stood down on the whole commit
whenever MERGE_HEAD existed — "a merge is in progress, therefore this is not
authoring." That is one inference too many: a sync file hand-edited during a
conflict resolution is authoring wearing a merge's clothes, and the blanket exit
waved it straight through. The reviewer named the exact vector I had flagged as
"considered and rejected as over-built"; it was neither.

The allowance is now per-file and earned rather than assumed. A staged sync file
is inherited only if the index still matches what the merge brought in; anything
the working tree changed on top of MERGE_HEAD stays a violation. So a real
`git merge origin/dev` passes, an edit made on top of it does not, and the verdict
is per-file rather than per-commit — one merge can legitimately carry an inherited
file and reject an edited sibling.

Failure is closed, not open: if MERGE_HEAD is absent or the diff fails, the
inherited set stays empty and every staged sync file is treated as authored. A
guard that cannot prove a file was inherited must not assume it.

Env-arm ordering preserved as required — the NEO_SYNC_AUTOCOMMIT arm still runs
first, so a merge in flight cannot become a way to smuggle source into a sync
commit.

Three specs added, all red against the blanket-exit version this replaces:
hand-edited-during-merge rejected; inherited-passes-while-edited-sibling-rejected
(proving the per-file verdict); and mixed autocommit+merge rejected (pinning the
ordering).

Evidence: 12/12 green. Red-then-green confirmed by stashing the discriminator —
both hand-edit specs fail against the blanket exit, which is the hole this closes.
One of them failed on first authoring for a better reason than I expected: my
`stageFile` helper rewrites identical bytes, so "re-staging" produced no
divergence and the discriminator correctly called it inherited. The test was
wrong, not the code; it now writes real edits."
- 2026-07-16T20:18:22Z @neo-opus-grace cross-referenced by PR #15304
- 2026-07-16T20:48:59Z @tobiu referenced in commit `0c25cdc` - "fix(build): check-whitespace is merge-blind too — #15300 only half-unblocked the dev-merge (#15303) (#15304)

* fix(build): check-whitespace is merge-blind too — #15300 only half-unblocked the dev-merge (#15303)

#15300 made the sync-data guard merge-aware and its PR body claimed that unblocks
dev-refresh for every feature branch. It does not, and this is the correction.
Going to land #15272's roster join the moment #15300 merged, the dev-merge failed
again — the sync guard passed, and `check-whitespace` refused eight lines of
`resources/content/discussions/chunk-1/discussion-15173.md`: pipeline-authored,
carried in by the merge, never touched by the branch.

That whitespace is CORRECT. It is how markdown encodes a hard line break, so
GitHub discussion bodies are full of it, and the pipeline knows — its own workflow
reads `--no-verify: generated data fails whitespace hooks`. That escape belongs to
a trusted CI job committing generated data; it does not extend to a maintainer who
later INHERITS those commits through a merge, which is everyone. `check-whitespace`
runs on lint-staged's `*` glob — every staged file of every type — so it was always
going to inherit the same defect the moment a merge staged the pipeline's content.

The rule is extracted rather than copied. #15300 put the discriminator inline; a
second inline copy would be a duplicated primitive, and the copy that drifts is the
one nobody notices — it fails open, silently, on exactly the content it polices.
`mergeInheritance.mjs` now owns it and both guards consume it, so the semantics get
decided once: a staged file is inherited only while the index still matches what the
merge brought in, and it fails CLOSED — outside a merge, or if git cannot answer,
every file is treated as authored.

Fixing the fixture was the extraction's real cost, and worth recording: the existing
chore-sync spec copies its script into a temp repo, so the new import could not
resolve and 12 specs went red. The spec caught the coupling immediately; the fixture
now carries the helper.

Five whitespace specs pin the boundary: an ordinary commit still fails (the guard is
intact); an inherited file passes; a file hand-edited during the merge still fails,
because authoring in a merge is still authoring; inherited and authored files are
judged per-file inside ONE merge; and absolute paths resolve like relative ones,
since lint-staged passes absolute while git reports relative.

Evidence: 17/17 green across both guards. The end-to-end proof — an actual
`git merge origin/dev` on a name-prefixed branch — is #15303's AC and follows on this
head, since it is the check #15299 could not discharge and #15300 only half-delivered.

Resolves #15303 · Refs #15281

* fix(build): -z the MERGE_HEAD diff — quoted paths made the guard fail OPEN (#15303)

Cross-family RC, accepted whole. The reviewer attacked the seam I flagged as the
one I trusted least and found two real defects in it.

`git diff --cached --name-only` C-QUOTES any path carrying a tab, a space, a quote
or a non-ASCII byte (`"a\tb.md"`), and would split a newline-bearing name into two
entries. Those mangled strings match no candidate, so the file read as NOT diverged
— inherited — and the guard SKIPPED it. That is a fail-OPEN, on precisely the paths
hardest to eyeball, in a guard whose whole job is to not be fooled. Worse than the
block it replaces. `-z` emits raw NUL-terminated paths with no quoting; the parse
splits on NUL.

Second: `path.relative` yields the PLATFORM separator while git always reports `/`.
On Windows every candidate arrived as `a\b.md`, matched nothing, read as authored,
and blocked every merge on that platform. The candidate is now normalised onto
git's vocabulary — never the git output, which is already correct.

TWO WITNESSES WERE WRITTEN AND DELETED FOR BEING VACUOUS, which is the part worth
recording. Both were green, and both proved nothing:

  · an INHERITED tab-bearing path — absent from the diverged set either way, quoted
    or not, so the fixed and un-fixed helpers agree. Same verdict, different reason;
    it cannot discriminate.
  · a Windows-separator candidate — on POSIX `path.sep` IS `/`, so the normalisation
    is an identity no-op and the assertion cannot fail on this CI.

Shipping them would have dressed the Win32 arm in a green test it never earned. It
is reasoned, not proven, and the spec now says so in place. The surviving witness is
the authored-tab case: it reproduces the fail-open and goes red against the un-fixed
helper.

Evidence: 18/18 across both guards; red-then-green re-confirmed by stashing the
helper — the authored-tab witness fails, the rest hold.

Refs #15281"
- 2026-07-16T20:53:31Z @neo-opus-grace referenced in commit `4dec109` - "feat(fleet): join the throttle axis onto the consumed roster DTO — RA-1 (#15272)

The carried review action: the ticket requires the roster/detail DTO field and
says it feeds the same assembly as the wake axis. `FleetManager.fleetThrottleStatus()`
answering correctly proved nothing about the DTO a consumer actually reads — the
producer had no consumer, so the axis existed and was invisible.

Stacked on #15280's approved head (fc74f0f69a) rather than waiting on its merge,
per the reviewer's offered rebase-stack. The rebase conflicted only where both
axes append to the same seam (FleetManager imports + its spec's describe blocks);
both producers are additive, so both survive.

`createFleetCockpitStatus` gains `throttleStatus`, held to the SAME closed-enum
contract the wake axis was just repaired to honour: row `state` never leaves
`none | overage | rate-limited | unknown`, absence included. The wiring fact
lives on `sources.throttle` + the capability envelope, never in the observation
field — a fifth value would force every consumer to re-derive the taxonomy, which
is exactly the defect the wake repair removed one commit earlier. `fleetRoster()`
reads both axes concurrently: they share no state, so serializing them would add
the slower one's latency to every roster read.

Coverage lands where nothing existed. `fleetRoster` had NO test at all — which is
why a working producer verb could sit beside an unjoined DTO without anything
going red. The real assembler is now exercised end to end for wired, degraded,
and absent producers, plus the independence of the two axes. The Body-side source
labels are pinned against the Brain-side producer constants: they are duplicated
by design (this pure map never imports `ai/`), the join keys rows by that exact
string, and matching today is not the same as pinned.

Evidence: 502/502 fleet unit specs green (484 before this join)."
- 2026-07-16T21:17:55Z @tobiu referenced in commit `97e0f33` - "feat(fleet): throttle-state read producer — the honest axis (#15272) (#15281)

* feat(fleet): throttle-state read producer — the honest axis (#15272)

The ticket's first design task was naming a trustworthy throttle truth
source; the binding conclusion is that none exists yet — every candidate
falsified at source (lifecycle failureReason is content-generic and a
rate-limited harness keeps running; stderr is byte-counts only by
secret-hygiene design; A2A self-reports are prose; provider usage APIs
need credentials+polling; harness session files are heterogeneous). Per
the AC's fail-honest clause the adapter ships the contract and the seam:
taxonomy clamping (no fifth state can smuggle past the telltale), the
capability envelope (we-cannot-see stays distinguishable from
nothing-is-throttled), and the injected resolveThrottleState reader the
future watchdog-signals producer flips. Platform default: every row
honestly unknown under degraded/none — never a fabricated none.

FleetManager.fleetThrottleStatus() exposes the roster snapshot turnkey
via the throttleStateOptions plain-field seam.

* fix(fleet): throttle producer repair — freshness contract + counted degradation + redaction (#15272)

RA-2 of the cycle-1 review: (1) the freshness bound is now DEFINED at the
contract — the reader resolves synchronously within the snapshot, so the
envelope's capturedAt IS the observation-time bound for every row; an
async-sampling future producer must resolve in-call or answer unknown,
and pre-sampled staleness is the producer's declared residual, never this
adapter's to hide. (2) Explicit-unknown and out-of-contract answers are
now DISTINCT all the way into the capability: invalid values are counted
and degrade to partial with a named reason — a producer-contract
violation can no longer hide under wired/observed (the silent clamp that
laundered garbage into honest-unknown is gone). (3) Diagnostics are
row-local and REDACTED before Body projection (token shapes + secret
vocabulary; contract-identical with the wake sibling — consolidating the
shared helper rides the post-merge branch alignment, stated at the
function).

* feat(fleet): join the throttle axis onto the consumed roster DTO — RA-1 (#15272)

The carried review action: the ticket requires the roster/detail DTO field and
says it feeds the same assembly as the wake axis. `FleetManager.fleetThrottleStatus()`
answering correctly proved nothing about the DTO a consumer actually reads — the
producer had no consumer, so the axis existed and was invisible.

Stacked on #15280's approved head (fc74f0f69a) rather than waiting on its merge,
per the reviewer's offered rebase-stack. The rebase conflicted only where both
axes append to the same seam (FleetManager imports + its spec's describe blocks);
both producers are additive, so both survive.

`createFleetCockpitStatus` gains `throttleStatus`, held to the SAME closed-enum
contract the wake axis was just repaired to honour: row `state` never leaves
`none | overage | rate-limited | unknown`, absence included. The wiring fact
lives on `sources.throttle` + the capability envelope, never in the observation
field — a fifth value would force every consumer to re-derive the taxonomy, which
is exactly the defect the wake repair removed one commit earlier. `fleetRoster()`
reads both axes concurrently: they share no state, so serializing them would add
the slower one's latency to every roster read.

Coverage lands where nothing existed. `fleetRoster` had NO test at all — which is
why a working producer verb could sit beside an unjoined DTO without anything
going red. The real assembler is now exercised end to end for wired, degraded,
and absent producers, plus the independence of the two axes. The Body-side source
labels are pinned against the Brain-side producer constants: they are duplicated
by design (this pure map never imports `ai/`), the join keys rows by that exact
string, and matching today is not the same as pinned.

Evidence: 502/502 fleet unit specs green (484 before this join)."
